title: Button Component
content:
## Button Component This component provides a customizable and accessible button element for the DocMed UI. ### Purpose The Button component is a foundational UI element that allows users to trigger actions. It is designed to be flexible, supporting various visual styles (variants) and sizes. ### Key Exports * **`Button`**: The main React component for rendering a button. It accepts standard button attributes and specific props for styling and behavior. * **`buttonVariants`**: A `class-variance-authority` function that generates CSS classes based on `variant` and `size` props. This is useful for external customization or when building custom button elements. ### Props All standard HTML button attributes are supported, in addition to the following: * **`variant`** (`'default' | 'destructive' | 'outline' | 'secondary' | 'ghost' | 'link'`): Defines the visual style of the button. Defaults to `'default'`. * `default`: The primary button style. * `destructive`: For actions that can have negative consequences. * `outline`: A bordered button with a transparent background. * `secondary`: A less prominent button style. * `ghost`: A button with no background or border, appearing on hover. * `link`: A text-based button that looks like a hyperlink. * **`size`** (`'default' | 'sm' | 'lg' | 'icon'`): Defines the dimensions of the button. Defaults to `'default'`. * `default`: Standard button size. * `sm`: Small button size. * `lg`: Large button size. * `icon`: A button sized for an icon only. * **`asChild`** (`boolean`): If `true`, the component will render as a `Slot` from `@radix-ui/react-slot`, allowing for composition and extending the button's functionality with other components. * **`className`**: Allows adding custom CSS classes to the button. ### Usage Example ```tsx import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'; function MyComponent() { return (
); } ``` ### Customization with `buttonVariants` You can use `buttonVariants` directly to create custom button-like elements: ```tsx import { buttonVariants } from '@/components/ui/button'; function CustomButton() { return ( Custom Styled Span ); } ``` ### Accessibility The Button component incorporates accessibility best practices, including `focus-visible` styles and proper handling of disabled states.
